heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 26 January 2025 with a total accumulation of 32.56mm over 22.4 hours and falling at a rate of 6.1mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 14.3 hour storm when 20.15mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 36.3 hours on 27 January 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 10.72mm with a maximum intensity of 20.3mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 14 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 15 days.

top_10_rainy.status: This period, starting 09 November 2025, is ranked 8th among the longest rainy periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 7 days so far.  The longest rainy period started on 19 January 2025 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 6 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 6 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (180°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 49.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.1°C) and outside temperature (down 1.8°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 318 m) and rainfall (down 0.1 mm) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 85.0m higher than yesterday, 68.0m lower than the past week, 318.5m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 561m to 715m and averaged 696.6m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; you can expect comfortable, mild temperatures. Also, humidity should not be a concern and near calm conditions. Showers, overcast skies with low cloud, and periods of sunshine are expected. Low pressure may bring unsettled weather.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (92%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
